behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename detection plus CLI/report wiring. MOCK-MISS: this is not just a local function check; rename status must flow from git output into category aggregation. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: rename classification depends on real git diff/name-status behavior and subprocess output format, which in-process fakes can misrepresent and let bugs pass. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text has no AI/ML decision step.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit baseline, perform a real rename, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert report shows the file only in `renamed` with zero corresponding add/delete entries for that path.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real binary content handling through git and CLI I/O. REAL-INFRA: binary diff behavior and subprocess stdout/stderr interactions are OS/process-level. MOCK-HIDE: mocked diff text often omits real binary markers/encoding edge cases that cause decode crashes. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no model output quality or multi-agent sequence is involved.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, add/modify a true binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI subprocess, assert zero crash/exit success and that binary changes are included in counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at the git/CLI boundary for unborn HEAD repositories. REAL-INFRA: real git commands on no-commit repos return specific exit codes/errors that mocks can smooth over. INTEGRATION-BRAKE(a): yes, real subprocess behavior is required, so Integration is too low. Rejection gate: no AI/ML dependency, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, invoke CLI subprocess, assert successful handling and an empty structured report instead of traceback/non-zero failure.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against real repository history. MOCK-MISS: bug can emerge when multiple modules (history reader, classifier, reporter) interact over real git data. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: true git history semantics and subprocess responses are needed; synthetic fixtures can miss ordering and status edge cases. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow disqualified because behavior is deterministic SCM accounting, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real git repo history with known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run CLI subprocess, and assert category counts exactly equal independently computed ground truth from git history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is git index vs working-tree semantics observed through real CLI execution. REAL-INFRA: staged/unstaged state is a real git repository state machine and subprocess interaction. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing repository responses can miss index/worktree discrepancies that appear only with real git. Rejection gate: no AI/ML step, so Agentic/Workflow are not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, create equivalent change sets once as staged and once as unstaged, run CLI subprocess for each mode, and assert identical structured output.
